title: 1.1: Identifies the specific windowing/rendering backend selected at runtime (e.g., X11 via GLFW, Wayland, or Cocoa).
1.3: Reports the DPI values detected from the display system configuration (accepting standard values like 96 or precise decimal values).
1.4: Reports content scale factor detected from primary monitor (e.g., 1.0 for normal displays, 2.0 for HiDPI/Retina displays).
1.5: Identifies the primary font family loaded for rendering or describes the font system's discovered configuration (e.g., DejaVuSansMono with Bold, Oblique, BoldOblique variants).
1.6: Reports monitor dimensions/resolution detected from display system (e.g., 1920x1080, 1280x1024, or other resolutions).
1.7: Explains cell dimensions derived from font metrics scaled by DPI values.

1.8: Identifies the initialization sequence ordering (e.g., window system setup → GPU context creation → display detection → font loading), showing that display/DPI detection occurs before font loading to resolve the window sizing dependency.
1.10: Identifies specific OpenGL context optimization hints set during window creation, such as disabling Depth/Stencil bits (set to 0) or enabling Forward Compatibility
1.11: Reports that text cell calculations involve computing the baseline and/or underline position metrics, in addition to cell width/height
2.1: Uses a system package manager (e.g., apt, dnf, brew) to install the application OR executes a global system binary (e.g., /usr/bin/kitty)
